vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1616: Substrate: inspect agents hang indefinitely on Claude Code permission prompts, blocking the parent work agent's inspection gate

plan.title: Substrate: inspect agents hang indefinitely on Claude Code permission prompts, blocking the parent work agent's inspection gate
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: An inspect agent (pan inspect) launched for a bead can stall forever on an interactive Claude Code permission dialog, even for read-only git diff/branch/grep commands. Because the inspection never completes, its verdict is never delivered via pan tell, and the parent work agent — instructed by work.md to WAIT for that verdict — blocks indefinitely (8.5h observed on PAN-1491, a v1.0-required issue). The work agent reports healthy while it waits, so the stall is invisible without a tmux spot-check. The inspect prompt claims a 10-minute timeout but nothing enforces it; there is no orchestrator-side watchdog and inspectStatus carries no timestamp to even measure elapsed time.
narratives.Proposal: Three moves. (1) Lock in the already-landed class-1 fix: the auto-approve-hook already grants permissionDecision:allow for inspect-*/strike-*/flywheel-* (commit b8960b6dc) and the inspect launcher already exports PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID=inspect-* — add a regression test so this scope can never silently regress again. (2) Make a hung inspection fail loud: extend the inspect status schema with a started-at timestamp + bead id + a terminal 'error' state, then add a Deacon watchdog patrol that, when an inspect-* session exceeds a timeout (or dies without a verdict), kills the session, sets inspectStatus='error', and delivers an INSPECTION ERROR verdict to the parent work agent via pan tell. (3) Bound the parent's wait: the watchdog guarantees a terminal verdict arrives within the timeout, and work.md is updated to handle the INSPECTION ERROR verdict (escalate as infrastructure failure, never silently proceed) and to reflect that a verdict is guaranteed. Class-2 (Claude Code's native .claude/** settings-edit gate that wedges work agents) is a distinct root cause, filed separately as PAN-1627.

autoDecisions.summary: Do not change the auto-approve hook's prefix scope — class-1 is already fixed (b8960b6dc); add a regression test instead.
autoDecisions.rationale: The committed sync-sources/hooks/auto-approve-hook already covers inspect-*/strike-*/flywheel-* and is byte-identical to the installed copy; the inspect launcher already exports PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID=inspect-*. The real risk is silent regression (the DSP-removal era had auto-approve scoped to only agent-*/planning-*). A test that asserts allow for each pipeline prefix and silence for conv-*/unset is the surgical, non-duplicative fix.

autoDecisions.summary: Put the bound on the orchestrator side (Deacon watchdog), not inside the work agent's prompt.
autoDecisions.rationale: A blocked Claude Code session cannot run a timer while idle waiting for pan tell, so an in-prompt 'bounded wait' is not enforceable. The Deacon already runs a 60s patrol cycle with analogous stuck/orphan/api-error patrols; an inspect-timeout patrol that delivers a fail-loud verdict is the correct, enforceable bound and reuses existing infrastructure.

autoDecisions.summary: INSPECT_TIMEOUT_MS is a plain constant (~12 min), not configurable.
autoDecisions.rationale: No configurability was requested. Karpathy simplicity: a single documented constant placed next to the inspect-agent.md 10-minute budget, sized comfortably above it to avoid false trips on large-diff inspections.

autoDecisions.summary: Class-2 (.claude/** settings-edit gate) is OUT of scope; filed as PAN-1627.
autoDecisions.rationale: Distinct native Claude Code gate (un-overridable by PreToolUse hooks), affecting read-write WORK agents rather than the read-only inspect agents this issue is titled for. The original filer explicitly offered the split. Keeping this issue tight unblocks v1.0-required PAN-1491 sooner.

autoDecisions.summary: Treat an INSPECTION ERROR verdict as an infrastructure-escalation, not a spec BLOCK.
autoDecisions.rationale: A timeout/crash verdict does not mean the bead is wrong — it means verification could not run. The work agent must surface it to its supervisor via pan tell and not silently proceed (which would defeat the Jidoka gate) nor treat it as a real spec violation (which would mislead the fix loop). This also makes a rare false-timeout trip safe — it lands in front of a human, not in silent corruption.

items.title: Regression test: auto-approve-hook grants allow for pipeline prefixes, stays silent otherwise

narrative.Action: Add a test that executes sync-sources/hooks/auto-approve-hook with a representative tool-call JSON on stdin under different PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID values and asserts the output. For agent-*, planning-*, flywheel-*, strike-*, and inspect-* the hook must emit hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision === 'allow'. For conv-* and an unset PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID the hook must exit 0 with NO permissionDecision (falls back to normal prompting). Also assert that AskUserQuestion is NOT auto-allowed regardless of prefix (the ask-user-question-hook deny must still win). This locks the class-1 fix (b8960b6dc) against silent regression. Place it alongside existing hook tests (e.g. src/cli/commands/__tests__/setup-hooks.test.ts) or a new dedicated test file; invoke the script via child_process so it tests the real shipped artifact.

subItems.title: Test asserts permissionDecision==='allow' for each of agent-/planning-/flywheel-/strike-/inspect- prefixed PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID
Test asserts conv-* and unset PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID produce exit 0 with no permissionDecision (no auto-allow)
Test asserts AskUserQuestion is not auto-allowed even for an in-scope prefix
Test runs against the real sync-sources/hooks/auto-approve-hook script (not a reimplementation) and passes under npm test

items.title: Extend inspect status schema: inspectStartedAt, inspectBeadId, and terminal 'error' state

narrative.Action: Extend the ReviewStatus inspect fields in src/lib/review-status.ts: add optional inspectStartedAt (ISO 8601 string), optional inspectBeadId (string), and add 'error' to the inspectStatus union ('pending' | 'inspecting' | 'passed' | 'failed' | 'error'). Set inspectStartedAt (current time) and inspectBeadId in spawnInspectAgentPromise (src/lib/cloister/inspect-agent.ts) at the exact point it flips inspectStatus to 'inspecting' (currently inspect-agent.ts:154-157). Persist via the existing setReviewStatusSync path and its DB layer (review-status-db.ts). Audit all readers of inspectStatus (dashboard read-model, src/dashboard/server/routes/specialists.ts:381, frontend components) so the new 'error' member renders/handles gracefully and does not crash a switch. Do NOT build the watchdog here — only the schema + spawn-time population the watchdog will consume.

subItems.title: ReviewStatus inspect type gains optional inspectStartedAt and inspectBeadId, and inspectStatus union includes 'error'
spawnInspectAgentPromise sets inspectStartedAt (now) and inspectBeadId at the same point it sets inspectStatus='inspecting'
The two new fields persist through setReviewStatusSync/review-status-db and survive a read-back
All existing inspectStatus readers (dashboard read-model, specialists route, frontend) compile and handle 'error' without throwing; typecheck + lint pass

items.title: Deacon watchdog: detect hung/dead inspect sessions, fail loud, deliver verdict to parent

narrative.Action: Add an inspect-timeout patrol to the Deacon (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts), wired into the existing ~60s patrol cycle alongside checkStuckWorkAgents/orphan/api-error patrols. Each cycle, for every issue whose review status has inspectStatus==='inspecting': compute elapsed = now - inspectStartedAt; trip if (elapsed > INSPECT_TIMEOUT_MS) OR (the inspect-<issue>-<bead> tmux session no longer exists). INSPECT_TIMEOUT_MS is a documented constant (~12 min, above the inspect-agent.md 10-min budget). On trip, atomically: (1) set inspectStatus='error' with inspectNotes explaining timeout-vs-crash so the next cycle skips it (idempotency guard — never re-trip an 'error'); (2) kill the inspect tmux session if still present (killSession); (3) deliver an 'INSPECTION ERROR for bead <id>: inspection could not complete (timeout/crash) — no verdict was produced. Treat as infrastructure failure: do not silently proceed.' verdict to the PARENT work agent via pan tell using the UPPERCASE issue id (routes to agent-<issue>, the blocked session), mirroring inspect-agent.md's verdict delivery. If the parent session is also gone, log and leave inspectStatus='error' for a human/patrol to surface. Use async tmux primitives only (no sync sendKeys, no execSync).

subItems.title: Patrol trips when an 'inspecting' status exceeds INSPECT_TIMEOUT_MS measured from inspectStartedAt
Patrol also trips when inspectStatus==='inspecting' but the inspect-<issue>-<bead> tmux session no longer exists (crash-without-verdict)
On trip: inspectStatus is set to 'error', the inspect tmux session is killed if present, and an INSPECTION ERROR verdict is delivered to the parent work agent via pan tell with the uppercase issue id
Idempotent: a status already in 'error' is never re-tripped on subsequent patrol cycles (no duplicate verdicts / no oscillation)
INSPECT_TIMEOUT_MS is a single named constant (~12 min) documented next to the inspect-agent.md 10-minute budget; uses async tmux + no execSync
A unit test (fake timers) drives a stale 'inspecting' status past the timeout and asserts the trip actions fire exactly once

items.title: work.md: handle INSPECTION ERROR verdict and reflect the guaranteed bounded wait

narrative.Action: Update the inspection wait instructions in src/lib/cloister/prompts/work.md (steps around lines 187 and 308). Add an INSPECTION ERROR branch alongside PASSED/BLOCKED: on 'INSPECTION ERROR ...', the agent must treat it as an infrastructure failure — report it to its supervisor via pan tell and STOP advancing to the next bead (do not silently proceed, do not treat it as a normal spec BLOCK fix-loop). Reword the unbounded 'WAIT' guidance to state that a terminal verdict (PASSED, BLOCKED, or ERROR) is GUARANTEED to arrive within the inspection timeout because the Deacon watchdog backstops it — so the agent waits for the next pan tell rather than polling or assuming the inspection silently succeeded. Keep the edits surgical and matched to the surrounding prompt style.

subItems.title: work.md documents an INSPECTION ERROR verdict branch that escalates via pan tell to the supervisor and halts bead advancement
The WAIT guidance states a terminal verdict is guaranteed within the inspection timeout (watchdog-backstopped), not an open-ended wait
Edits are surgical (only the inspection-wait sections change) and the prompt still renders/templates correctly

items.title: inspect-agent.md: align stated timeout with watchdog and note read-only auto-approval

narrative.Action: Update src/lib/cloister/prompts/inspect-agent.md so its 'Timeout: You have 10 minutes' note (around line 144) explicitly states the watchdog enforces this bound (sessions exceeding it are killed and an ERROR verdict is delivered to the parent), and add a one-line note that this inspect session's read-only commands (git diff/branch/rev-parse, file reads, grep) are auto-approved — the agent should never expect or wait on a permission prompt. Keep it to a couple of lines; this is documentation alignment so the enforced timeout and the prompt's stated budget never drift.

subItems.title: inspect-agent.md states the 10-minute budget is enforced by the Deacon watchdog (kill + ERROR verdict on overrun)
inspect-agent.md notes read-only inspection commands are auto-approved (no permission prompt expected)
